fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 60 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False and skipped when `clamp` is True. It should be `if clamp:` (or equivalently, the body should execute when clamp is True), not `if not clamp:`.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True (default), the branch `if not clamp` should NOT be entered — clamping logic should execute when clamp is True, not when it is False.
The then-branch (clamping code) should only execute when clamp is False, but the intent is it should execute when clamp is True — this branch being entered with clamp=True is the bug.
y__2 (the clamped value) is only computed in the `if not clamp` branch, but clamping should happen when clamp is True, so this assignment should not exist when clamp is False.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamping is enabled (clamp=True), the returned value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
